pundit.name: Alexi Lalas
pundit.take: **Devin, look at me. LOOK AT ME. You drafted Bosnia!** Now, let's get to this steaming pile of World Cup lunacy we call the fantasy pool. We've got Norway, led by "Ghost Pepper" Devin, up against the might of France repped by "Bubba G" Gunner. Norway's swimming upstream these days, which is exactly what you get when you draft the USA and Haiti expecting World Cup glory. Devin, I don't know if you thought this was a youth soccer tournament in Des Moines, but this is the World Cup! Meanwhile, Gunner, your faith in the DR Congo was almost as heartfelt as your failure to pick up on any advancement points. But let me tell you something, France is going to bulldoze Norway, and it’ll be about as one-sided as the 1994 US team hosting the world. While Devin sits at the top without a single upset — a testament to his pedestrian roster — he’ll learn that holding first place with no strategy is like sitting on a throne of sand. Gunner’s France is going to teach him what real grit is all about, and that's my bombastic prediction. Learn and learn well, Devin: you are not cut out for this.

pundit_takes.headline: Ghost Pepper Devin Poised for Spicy Showdown Against Bubba G's France
pundit_takes.subtitle: Devin promises to turn up the heat with Norway's flair.

pundit_takes.headline: Bubba G Aims for Redemption Following Rafe's Tactical German Misstep
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gunner hopes Egypt can outperform high school level geography.

pundit_takes.headline: Mustard Boy Zach Relies on Saudi Arabia to Squeeze Past Cape Verde
pundit_takes.subtitle: Zach confident in Saudi Arabia, even if nobody else is.

pundit_takes.headline: Backpass Assassin Gayden Celebrates as Rafe's Ecuador Outsmarts Germany
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gayden takes notes as his son triumphs through beginner's luck.

rome_analytics_quotes: Rafe's chaos reigns at 15, leaving seasoned veterans clutching their spreadsheets in disbelief.
Rafe drafted three non-qualifiers and still rides second place—draft disaster genius!
Rafe splits the series with dad, giving Thanksgiving dinner a competitive edge.
Sim genius can't predict a 15-year-old making Gunner's odds drop—classic underestimation!
